system.description: A candle is a pillar-shaped light source made of wax or tallow. When lit, a candle sheds bright light out to a 5 radius and dim light to another 10 (15 total), burning for 6 turns (1 hour). Wax candles burn cleanly, but tallow candles produce a foul smelling smoke. Adventurers carrying tallow candles will never surprise creatures with acute olfaction.
